blockquote.p: Note to Balloters: The substantive change in this ballot broadens how an operation identifies its target and its parameter types: OperationDefinition.resource and OperationDefinition.parameter.type are now typed as uri rather than code, so operations can be defined against logical models identified by a canonical URI, not only resource-type codes. The remaining OperationDefinition changes are narrative: escape-valve guidance for kind and parameter.binding.strength, a jurisdiction-status clarification, and editorial corrections.

ul.li: OperationDefinition.resource retyped from code to uri so operations can target logical models identified by canonical URI [FHIR-51651]
OperationDefinition.parameter.type retyped from code to uri so a parameter can be typed against a logical model by URI [FHIR-51651]
Clarified handling for OperationDefinition.kind values outside the bound value set (escape-valve guidance) [FHIR-55176]
Clarified handling for OperationDefinition.parameter.binding.strength values outside the bound value set (escape-valve guidance) [FHIR-55178]
Jurisdiction elements are no longer deprecated but retain a clarifying deprecation note in the resource narrative [FHIR-53766]
Spelling and abbreviation corrections applied to the OperationDefinition narrative [FHIR-55175]

div.p: The OperationDefinition resource provides a formal computable definition of an operation or a named query. The OperationDefinition serves two principal purposes:

ul.li: To allow for automatic determination of system compatibility
To allow for dynamic generation of forms to drive the operations

div.p: See below for further information about these, and about how operations and named queries are executed.

OperationDefinitions are published to define operations that servers can implement in a common fashion. The FHIR specification itself describes some (see below), and other organizations (including IHE, national programs, jurisdictions and vendors) are able to publish additional OperationDefinitions.
OperationDefinition resources are referred to from two different places:

ul.li: From a CapabilityStatement, to declare what operations a system does or should implement

From another OperationDefinition resource. This allows for a server to describe a limited implementation of a standard operation, or to allow traceability if the server has to rename the operation due to a name clash

div.p: OperationDefinitions can be used to define a custom query (kind = query, discussed below) which functions as a special kind of search. This is different to a SearchParameter because a custom query may define multiple parameters and can define of complex search behavior beyond what SearchParameter can specify. It also typically overrides the behavior of the query functionality, while search parameters can only act as additional filters.
